page: Amazon Web Services c5d.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, which translates to a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance type integrates 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 94558.67 and a PassMark CPU score of 62263.66. The system demonstrates high memory bandwidth, reaching 231.4 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 97.89 GB/sec for writes. Database and web serving capabilities are also highly rated, as evidenced by 81.12 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 45.17 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are comparatively lower, with the medium 7B model achieving only 5.33 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5d.24xlarge offers high compute density and fast local storage, making it a viable option for workloads requiring rapid I/O operations without the overhead of external network storage. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio represents a tradeoff, limiting its suitability for memory-bound applications. This instance is best suited for compute-intensive workloads, software compilation, database operations, high-throughput web serving, and ray tracing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 3.6 GHz clock speed. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 25 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 62263.66 and high memory bandwidth, alongside robust database performance of 81.12 million Redis SET operations per second. However, LLM text generation is a notable bottleneck, with a medium 7B model yielding only 5.33 tokens per second. Qualitatively, the instance offers high compute and storage density, making it highly efficient for I/O-heavy processing but less optimal for memory-intensive tasks. It is best suited for software compilation, database operations, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for high-compute and database workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and fast local NVMe storage for I/O-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed
Bundled 3600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Tradeoff of lower performance in LLM text generation tasks
Best suited for software compilation, databases, and web serving
